title: Harden CopyQ and Espanso usage
goal: Turn existing CopyQ and Espanso installations into a safer operator convenience layer for clipboard history and standard snippets.

acceptance.assertion: CopyQ secret-handling rules are documented, including how to clear sensitive clipboard history.
Espanso snippets cover common PR/Bureau/evidence wording but contain no secrets and no dangerous mutation commands.
Existing service/autostart behavior is checked and does not interfere with terminal, tmux or SSH-Cockpit flows.

metadata.source_request: Evaluate every interesting utility tool and register the resulting plan in Bureau.
validation: Infra PR #107 GitHub CI validate passed.
scripts/utilities/heim-utilities-smoke.sh reported Backrest, Beszel, Stirling PDF, Paperless, Atuin, Difftastic, rga and LocalSend OK.
Restic util-base-v1 snapshot and single-file restore probe passed.
Paperless imported synthetic PDF document id 1 and indexed content containing UTIL-BASE-V1.
Stirling PDF converted the probe PDF to text and merged two copies into a 2-page PDF.
Docling 2.111.0 converted the probe PDF to Markdown and JSON with UTIL-BASE-V1 content.
metadata.outcome: Implemented in infra PR #107 and live-smoked on heim-pc; see runtime evidence for exact boundaries and remaining manual steps.

verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t010-1: CopyQ clear helper now requires --confirm; runtime evidence records one irreversible initial clear before guard was added.
verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t010-2: Espanso snippets contain no secrets and no mutating commands.
verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t010-3: Espanso systemd user service restarted and is active; CopyQ version observed.
